caption: Slice 2 of the nested atomic order queue (#826): a multi-unit drag-line form-up now builds a shared Order.Type.FORM_UP tag for the group instead of N unrelated standalone orders -- an internal representation change only, with NO intended difference in what a form-up looks like. Watch three regiments (Spearmen 140, Infantry 120, Archers 90) get box-selected, then a single right-drag sends each on its OWN march toward a distinct slice of the flank line -- exactly as before this slice: same per-unit destinations, same timing, still three separately visible march paths, not a single merged movement. Some units reshape their frontage and then march on staggered schedules (visible in the clip), a pre-existing reform-then-hold timing pattern this slice's order-tree change doesn't touch. Covered by new GUT tests asserting the shared FORM_UP parent/children wiring and a regression test proving the tree's cascade still stops correctly for a grouped order (Unit._advance_order_tree's hardened stop condition, a forward-looking guard, not a fix for a currently-reachable bug).
